---
title: "Paper Presentations"
sidebar: assignments
---


## Overview

Each student has been assigned one journal publication to present in class. The
expected structure of the paper presentations is outlined in this [Slideshow Template](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1hOlRmBfrAnXku6-Oq61sh0wkvnBqDTAw3tIEAXaStxw/edit). 
Students can use any slide show presentation software they wish, but should follow the 
presentation structure of the template.
A detailed presentation schedule is available in the internal [Course Planning Sheet](https://elearn.ucr.edu/courses/224081) sheet (see Columns Q-R of Projects Tab). 
The grading of both the paper and project presentations includes
anonymous feedback from all students as well as the instructor, where
understanding of the material, clarity of the oral presentations and critical
thinking are the main grading criteria. The grading forms will be provided in the 
Presentation Schedule (internal google sheet) shortly before the presentations start
on May 14th and May 19th. The following lists the assigned papers organized by 
course project topics.


## Publication list

All references in [Paperpile](https://paperpile.com/shared/GEN242-2026-fm6L56jC5QIiEwSUq3827kg)


Argelaguet R, Arnol D, Bredikhin D, Deloro Y, Velten B, Marioni JC, Stegle O (2020) MOFA+: a statistical framework for comprehensive integration of multi-modal single-cell data. Genome Biol 21: 111

Badia-I-Mompel P, Vélez Santiago J, Braunger J, Geiss C, Dimitrov D, Müller-Dott S, Taus P, Dugourd A, Holland CH, Ramirez Flores RO, et al (2022) decoupleR: ensemble of computational methods to infer biological activities from omics data. Bioinform Adv 2: vbac016

Bouhaddani SE, Houwing-Duistermaat J, Salo P, Perola M, Jongbloed G, Uh H-W (2016) Evaluation of O2PLS in Omics data integration. BMC Bioinformatics 17 Suppl 2: 11

Cantini L, Zakeri P, Hernandez C, Naldi A, Thieffry D, Remy E, Baudot A (2021) Benchmarking joint multi-omics dimensionality reduction approaches for the study of cancer. Nat Commun 12: 124

Chen T, Guestrin C (2016a) XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System. arXiv [csLG]. doi: 10.48550/arXiv.1603.02754

Chen T, Guestrin C (2016b) XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System. Proceedings of the 22nd ACM SIGKDD International Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining. ACM, New York, NY, USA, pp 785–794

Díaz-Uriarte R, Alvarez de Andrés S (2006) Gene selection and classification of microarray data using random forest. BMC Bioinformatics 7: 3

Griss J, Viteri G, Sidiropoulos K, Nguyen V, Fabregat A, Hermjakob H (2020) ReactomeGSA - Efficient Multi-Omics Comparative Pathway Analysis. Mol Cell Proteomics 19: 2115–2125

Gu Z, El Bouhaddani S, Pei J, Houwing-Duistermaat J, Uh H-W (2021) Statistical integration of two omics datasets using GO2PLS. BMC Bioinformatics 22: 131

Lundberg SM, Erion G, Chen H, DeGrave A, Prutkin JM, Nair B, Katz R, Himmelfarb J, Bansal N, Lee S-I (2020) From local explanations to global understanding with explainable AI for trees. Nat Mach Intell 2: 56–67

Meng C, Basunia A, Peters B, Gholami AM, Kuster B, Culhane AC (2019) MOGSA: Integrative single sample gene-set analysis of multiple omics data. Mol Cell Proteomics 18: S153–S168

Odom GJ, Ban Y, Colaprico A, Liu L, Silva TC, Sun X, Pico AR, Zhang B, Wang L, Chen X (2020) PathwayPCA: An R/Bioconductor package for pathway based integrative analysis of multi-omics data. Proteomics 20: e1900409

Pai S, Hui S, Isserlin R, Shah MA, Kaka H, Bader GD (2019) netDx: interpretable patient classification using integrated patient similarity networks. Mol Syst Biol 15: e8497

Schubert M, Klinger B, Klünemann M, Sieber A, Uhlitz F, Sauer S, Garnett MJ, Blüthgen N, Saez-Rodriguez J (2018) Perturbation-response genes reveal signaling footprints in cancer gene expression. Nat Commun 9: 20

Singh A, Shannon CP, Gautier B, Rohart F, Vacher M, Tebbutt SJ, Lê Cao K-A (2019) DIABLO: an integrative approach for identifying key molecular drivers from multi-omics assays. Bioinformatics 35: 3055–3062

Strobl C, Boulesteix A-L, Zeileis A, Hothorn T (2007) Bias in random forest variable importance measures: illustrations, sources and a solution. BMC Bioinformatics 8: 25
